content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 28, 2017

James Benjamin
Planning Commission
City of Half Moon Bay
400 Pilarcitos Avenue
Half Moon Bay, CA 94019-1475

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-17-211

Dear Mr. Benjamin:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Pursuant to our telephone conversation on September 26, 2017, we consider your advice request to be withdrawn.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Jack Woodside
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Zachary W. Norton
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ZWN:jgl

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: Mr. Benjamin sought advice regarding conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but subsequently withdrew his request.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC acknowledges that the advice request has been withdrawn pursuant to a telephone conversation on September 26, 2017.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no substantive QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document confirms withdrawal of the advice request rather than providing legal guidance. No details about the original question are included in the letter itself.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Benjamin sought advice regarding conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but subsequently withdrew his request.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges that the advice request has been withdrawn pursuant to a telephone conversation on September 26, 2017.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter from the FPPC dated September 28, 2017, confirming that James Benjamin's advice request regarding Political Reform Act conflict of interest provisions (File No. W-17-211) has been withdrawn following a telephone conversation.
